channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: On the Ground in Beirut
item.description: Our Beirut bureau chief, Christina Goldbaum, reports on the escalating conflict between Israel and the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah, as Israel’s military seizes areas of southern Lebanon and carries out bombings.
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Experts doubt Tehran’s claims of advanced ‘hypersonic’ missiles.
item.description: As Iran fires fewer ballistic missiles, experts doubt Tehran is saving more advanced weapons.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A military parade in Iran in 2024. Tehran has said that it has developed hypersonic missile technology, but many security experts doubt that the missiles have such capacity.

item.title: Pro-American Kurdish Forces Are Preparing Possible Iran Incursion
item.description: The C.I.A. has given small weapons to Iranian Kurdish forces in Iraq in a covert program that began before the current war.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Julian E. Barnes, Christiaan Triebert, Parin Behrooz and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A member of the Kurdistan Democratic Party of Iran inspects the damage sustained at the party’s Azadi Camp after an Iranian cross-border attack in the Kurdish region of northern Iraq.

item.title: Whose Airstrike Hit a Girls’ School in Iran? The U.S. Says It’s Still Investigating.
item.description: The strike was one of the deadliest attacks of the American-Israeli campaign against Iran so far.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Karoline Leavitt, the White House press secretary, was asked on Wednesday if the United States had conducted the airstrike on an Iranian school.

item.title: Israelis Celebrate Purim Amid Iranian Missile Attacks
item.description: The war did not stop ultra-Orthodox parts of Jerusalem from drinking and dancing for the Jewish holiday.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Celebrating Purim inside a bomb shelter in Tel Aviv on Monday.

item.title: As Attacks Spread, War With Iran Reaches Far Beyond Mideast
item.description: NATO downed a missile fired by Iran at Turkey, and a U.S. sub sank an Iranian Navy ship off Sri Lanka, thousands of miles from the war zone.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper, Lara Jakes and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Damage in Tehran has been extensive, including at the police station in the background on Wednesday.

item.title: Senate Thwarts Bid to Curb Trump’s War Powers on Iran
item.description: Nearly every Republican voted to block a measure that would require that President Trump win authorization from Congress to continue the offensive in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The rubble of a police station in Tehran this week after the U.S.-Israeli airstrikes.

item.title: Travelers Stranded by U.S.-Israel Conflict With Iran Face Endless Flight Cancellations
item.description: Even far from hubs like Dubai and Abu Dhabi, the disruptions from the growing violence have left people jumping “from one cancellation to the next.”
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung, Gabe Castro-Root and Niraj Chokshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tourists in Abu Dhabi, in the United Arab Emirates. on Sunday, the day after the United States and Israel struck Iran, setting off a conflict that has caused chaos for travelers.

item.title: Spain insists it is not cooperating with the U.S. on Iran war despite a White House claim.
item.description: It was part of a back
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.media:description: Pedro Sánchez, Spain’s prime minister, has condemned the attack on Iran as a violation of international law.

item.title: U.S. Submarine Launches Its First Torpedo in Combat Since World War II
item.description: Since 1991, Navy subs have launched scores of cruise missiles in combat, but the torpedo attack off Sri Lanka is a return to form after 80 years.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ishara S. Kodikara/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Health care workers transporting the bodies of Iranian sailors who were killed on the Iris Dena off the coast of Sri Lanka on Wednesday.

item.title: Hegseth will headline a campaign rally next week in the home district of a soldier killed in the Iran war.
item.description: The defense secretary planned to appear at a “Top Gun”-themed event for Representative Zach Nunn, Republican of Iowa, in the district where all four fallen soldiers had been stationed.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.media:description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth at the White House on Tuesday.

item.title: Videos Show Homes and Businesses Across Mideast Caught Up by War
item.description: As the conflict widens, residents across the Middle East are feeling the consequences of the U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Josh Holder, Leanne Abraham and Malika Khurana
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: A U.N. panel condemns the U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Nick Cumming-Bruce
item.media:description: A photo made available by the Iranian state-run news media of workers clearing debris at a school in Minab, Iran.

item.title: Here’s where ships and energy facilities have been damaged.
item.dc:creator: Blacki Migliozzi and Christiaan Triebert

item.title: Liquid Natural Gas Exporters Like Shell and ExxonMobil Could See Big Profits From War
item.description: Prices for liquefied natural gas have shot up in recent days, which could bring bigger profits for European and U.S. energy companies.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: A liquefied natural gas production facility in Qatar this week. Companies like Shell, TotalEnergies and ExxonMobil could benefit from the war.

item.title: The conflict is choking off the world’s oil and gas.
item.dc:creator: Blacki Migliozzi, Christiaan Triebert, Peter Eavis, Keith Collins, Jacqueline Gu and Rebecca Elliott

item.title: As Israel Pounds Lebanon, Thousands of Syrians There Flee Back Home
item.description: “I felt great fear and I ran,” says one of thousands of Syrians who are leaving what had been a relatively safe refuge during the war in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Reham Mourshed and Raja Abdulrahim
item.media:description: Smoke billowed on Wednesday after a strike in Khiam, Lebanon. The escalating conflict has prompted some refugees to return home to Syria.

item.title: Israel and U.S. Trumpet Their Collaboration in War Against Iran
item.description: U.S. and Israeli military officials are talking as often as 4,000 to 5,000 times a day, divvying up targets across Iran.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger, Eric Schmitt and Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke rose on Tuesday from an airstrike in Tehran. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said that having “such a capable ally” as Israel was “a breath of fresh air.”

item.title: U.S. Campaign in Iran Is ‘Far From Over,’ Joint Chiefs Chairman Says
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said responsibility for a strike on an Iranian girls’ school was under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People praying in front of the remains of a police station that was hit in an airstrike.

item.title: Hegseth Says U.S. Strike Killed an Iranian Who Plotted to Assassinate Trump
item.description: The Pentagon provided few details, but the Trump administration has said that Iran’s efforts to kill U.S. officials is one reason the United States launched its bombing campaign.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said that the leader of the Iranian unit that tried to kill President Trump was killed in a strike on Tuesday.

item.title: Here’s what satellite images show on efforts to destroy Iran’s ballistic missiles.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Granados, Daniel Wood and John Ismay
item.media:description: A building at a missile facility near Isfahan appears to be severely damaged.

item.title: The U.K. and France Deploy Some Forces to the Mideast Despite Misgivings About Iran Attacks
item.description: The two nations, as well as Greece, said they would send assets to the region. The Netherlands was weighing a request to join them.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier, which President Emmanuel Macron of France said would be deployed to help his country’s interests in the Middle East.

item.title: As Iran Expands Attacks, U.S. Orders More Diplomatic Staff to Leave Embassies in 4 Countries
item.description: On Tuesday, nonemergency staff and their family members were told to leave several posts as Iran continued to retaliate after U.S. and Israeli strikes.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Police standing guard near the U.S. Consulate in Karachi, Pakistan, on Wednesday, following violent demonstrations across the country.

item.title: NATO Air Defenses Shoot Down Iranian Missile Headed Toward Turkey
item.description: The Incirlik air base in southern Turkey hosts American forces but Turkey has said that it will not allow its airspace to be used for attacks on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard, Lara Jakes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ali Ihsan Ozturk/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Iranians crossing into Turkey in Van, Turkey, on Tuesday. Turkey, a NATO member, shares a 300-mile border with Iran.

item.title: U.S. Submarine Torpedoed Iranian Warship Off Sri Lanka as Conflict Widens
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said it was the first such strike since World War II. The Sri Lankan authorities said they had rescued 32 sailors from the crew of 180.
item.dc:creator: Pamodi Waravita, Anupreeta Das and Lynsey Chutel
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An ambulance at the naval headquarters in Galle, Sri Lanka, on Wednesday. Sri Lanka said it had rescued 32 critically injured sailors after their ship sank earlier in the day.

item.title: Iran Made Secret Outreach After Strikes, Highlighting Trump’s Challenge
item.description: President Trump is beginning to consider what sort of Iranian government might come next, as the U.S.-Israeli assault continues.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley, Julian E. Barnes and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Black smoke over Tehran following airstrikes on Tuesday.

item.title: On Iran, Vance Balances Between Trump and the Anti-Intervention Right
item.description: JD Vance long championed opposition to conflicts abroad. With President Trump all-in on war with Iran, the vice president is staying loyal even as allies make his qualms known.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance at the White House last month.

item.title: After U.S. Attacks on Iran, FIFA Grapples With How to Handle the World Cup
item.description: Soccer’s global governing body is grappling with a World Cup first: a tournament host going to war with a participating nation.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran’s national soccer team before a World Cup qualifying match in Tehran in March 2025.

item.title: Global Leaders Brace for the Fallout From a Fast Metastasizing War
item.description: Higher energy prices, political instability and a potential new wave of refugees: The escalating regional conflict in the Middle East could have far-reaching effects.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A woman cries after seeing the rubble from a building that was hit by airstrikes in Tehran on Tuesday.

item.title: Global Markets Swing as Investors Assess Energy Price Impact
item.description: Markets in Europe and the United States appeared to stabilize after days of turmoil, but stocks in Asia fell sharply.
item.dc:creator: Joe Rennison, River Akira Davis, Meaghan Tobin and Jason Karaian
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Traders in Seoul, where stocks plunged more than 10 percent on Wednesday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper, Farnaz Fassihi, Aaron Boxerman and Lara Jakes

item.title: Congress Splits Over Iran War as Senate Faces a Vote
item.description: The administration’s shifting justifications for the military operation alarmed Democrats, who said no clear rationale had been given. Republicans struggled to echo the evolving explanations.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spoke to reporters on Tuesday about the conflict with Iran.

item.title: Iran Strikes U.S. Military Communication Infrastructure in Mideast
item.description: Satellite images show damage near vital equipment on sites in at least five countries.
item.dc:creator: Devon Lum, Haley Willis and Riley Mellen
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Carney Says That Canada’s Support for Iran Airstrikes Came ‘With Regret’
item.description: The prime minister criticized Israel and the U.S. for not consulting other nations or the U.N. before their attack on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney, said Iran had persisted in its nuclear ambitions in defiance of years of diplomatic efforts, but he also criticized the United States and Israel.

item.title: Who Is Mojtaba Khamenei, a Son and Possible Successor of Iran’s Supreme Leader?
item.description: The selection of the son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in a strike on his compound on Saturday, could anger Iranians seeking change.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni, Sanam Mahoozi and Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Office of The Iranian Supreme Leader/Wana News Agency, via Reuters
item.media:description: A handout image from Iran state media of Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: These American Service Members Died in the Iran Conflict
item.description: They were from states across the country, in a unit dedicated to resupplying troops. One was wrapping up his final deployment and hoped to open a martial arts studio.
item.dc:creator: Corina Knoll, Christina Morales, Pooja Salhotra and Susan C. Beachy
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump Tries to Quiet Claims Among Supporters That Israel Dragged Him Into War
item.description: Many of President Trump’s allies have urged him and his Make America Great Again movement to shift away from their close ties to Israel and military entanglements in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump attempted to extinguish the idea that Israel forced the United States to enter a war with Iran.

item.title: Americans were urged to leave 14 countries in the region.
item.dc:creator: Lazaro Gamio
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Maps show wide-ranging attacks in the opening days of the conflict.
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Who’s in Charge of Iran’s Government After Khamenei’s Killing?
item.description: Who is in charge of Iran’s government after airstrikes killed the supreme leader and some of his defense and security officials? Erika Solomon, our bureau chief for Iran and Iraq, describes what we know.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Nikolay Nikolov, Jon Miller, Stephanie Swart, Rafaela Balster, Samuel Granados and Christina Shaman

item.title: How Trump Decided to Go to War
item.description: President Trump’s embrace of military action in Iran was set off by the determination of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel to end diplomatic negotiations. Our investigative reporter Mark Mazzetti describes how few of Mr. Trump’s advisers voiced opposition.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Leila Medina, Sutton Raphael, Joey Sendaydiego, Jon Hazell, Rafaela Balster and Whitney Shefte
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iranians Viewed by U.S. as Potential Leaders Died in Strikes, Trump Says
item.description: With the future of Iran deeply uncertain, President Trump acknowledged that it could end up with a ruler “as bad” as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley, Aaron Boxerman, Christina Goldbaum and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran after airstrikes on Tuesday. President Trump said that he had made the decision to go to war to pre-empt Iran, which he claimed was about to attack.

item.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s Son Emerges as Leading Choice to Be His Successor
item.description: If Mojtaba Khamenei is chosen by Iran’s senior clerics, it may signal a victory for hard-liners, an analyst said.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the leader of Iran, in Tehran in 2019.

item.title: Travelers, How Has the Iran Conflict Affected You? Tell Us Your Story.
item.description: Drones and missiles have closed airports and caused chaos across the Middle East since the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran on Saturday. We want to hear from affected travelers.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung, Ceylan Yeğinsu and Gabe Castro-Root
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Dubai International Airport, one of the world’s busiest hubs, has been mostly paralyzed since the United States and Israel began striking Iran on Saturday.

item.title: Nearly 900 People Have Been Killed in Fighting in the Middle East
item.description: The vast majority of deaths reported so far have been in Iran, but the human toll is being felt around the world.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke rising on Tuesday after a strike west of Tehran.

item.title: Trump Letter to Congress Justifying Iran Strikes Outlines No Imminent Threat
item.description: The president told lawmakers that the objective of the assault was to “neutralize Iran’s malign activities,” but did not present evidence of immediate plans by Tehran to attack the United States.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: A letter from President Trump submitted to Congress on Monday said the military attack on Iran was designed to “neutralize Iran’s malign activities.”

item.title: Rubio Walks Back Suggestion That Israel Forced U.S. Hand in Iran Strikes
item.description: A day earlier, the secretary of state had said that the decision to attack Iran was based on knowledge that Israel was going to strike, and an assumption that Iran would target U.S. forces as a result.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio speaking to reporters on Capitol Hill on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Navy Might Escort Oil and Gas Tankers in Persian Gulf
item.description: Shipping companies are not sending oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz because they fear their ships might be attacked.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump said the U.S. Navy was prepared to escort tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. The price of oil fell slightly after his announcement.
